title: The Acoustic Decay Ledger
hypothesis: Records as sound fragments in a spiral layout—each cell emits a tone on click that distorts as the record decays. Long-press paradoxically strengthens protection while degrading audio fidelity. sessionStorage ensures total loss on tab close, embodying ephemeral archiving.
dataModel: Seeded spiral-positioned record cells with audio frequency data, text fragments, and handling counters; mutation: acoustic-degradation-on-access
recordCountStrategy: Seeded from session timestamp: 7 + (timestamp % 13), yielding 7-19 variable records per session
interactionModel: Click plays tone and increments handling counter (corrupting frequency); long-press (800ms) toggles protection lock—locked records resist visual decay but audio distorts inversely; state persisted in sessionStorage, destroyed on tab close
stateModel: sessionStorage with per-record: {frequency, textFragment, handlingCount, protectionLevel, audioDistortion, visualDecay}; global: {spiralAngle, sessionSeed, totalAccessCount}
visualLanguage: Monochrome archival grid with spiral layout; records as muted rectangles with embossed text; degradation shown as character corruption (█░▓), SVG tear marks on edges; protected records get a wax-seal overlay that visually reinforces but audio distorts
expectedStructuralChange: Introduces Web Audio API as primary interaction modality; sessionStorage for ephemeral state; spiral spatial layout instead of grid; paradoxical protection mechanic (visual preservation vs. acoustic loss); variable record count per session
